title: Opponent-reaction and undercut-cover features
summary: Public F1 strategy sources emphasize that pit timing is driven by undercut/overcut windows, clean air, and whether a nearby rival has already stopped.
rationale: Public F1 strategy sources emphasize that pit timing is driven by undercut/overcut windows, clean air, and whether a nearby rival has already stopped. Recent pit-stop research also reports that a driver’s pit probability rises when the car ahead has pitted. Current best code has field context, but not explicit opponent-action signals.
implementation_hint: Within each race/lap, derive features keyed to nearby competitors: whether position-1 or position+1 pitted this lap or last lap, their compound and tyre life, local position cluster density, and a proxy `must_cover_undercut` flag when a nearby rival pits and the current driver has older tyres / worse lap delta. Use only information observable up to the current lap.
expected_effect: Adds strategy-reaction signal that raw self-history misses, especially around first-stop and cover-off decisions.
risk: You do not have exact time gaps, so position-adjacent proxies can be noisy on spread-out fields or safety-car-affected laps.
